      draftentityoperationcode      as Draftentityoperationcode,
      hasactiveentity               as Hasactiveentity,
      draftfieldchanges             as Draftfieldchanges
}

3. Behavior Definition

managed implementation in class zbp_i_so_hdr unique;
strict ( 2 );
with collaborative draft;
extensible;
define behavior for ZI_SO_HDR alias SalesHeader
persistent table zdb_so_hdr
extensible
draft table zdb_so_hdr01 query ZDD_QUERRY_01
etag master LastChangedAt
lock master total etag LastChangedAt
authorization master ( instance )
{
  field ( readonly, numbering : managed ) Soid;
  field ( readonly ) Sorder, CreatedBy, CreatedAt, LastChangedBy, LastChangedAt;
  field ( mandatory ) Partner, Status, Cuky;
  create;
  update;
  delete;

  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Discard;
  draft action Edit;
  draft action Resume;
  draft determine action Prepare;
  draft action Share;

  determination GenerateOrderNumber on save { create; }

  }
projection implementation in class zbp_i_so_hdr unique;
strict ( 2 );
extensible;
use collaborative draft;
use side effects;
define behavior for ZC_SO_HDR alias SalesHeader
extensible
{
  use create;
  use update;
  use delete;

  use action Edit;
  use action Activate;
  use action Discard;
  use action Resume;
  use action Prepare;
  use action Share;
}

4. Behavior Implementation class

CLASS zcl_psh_helper DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES: if_oo_adt_classrun.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS zcl_psh_helper IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.

    DATA: lt_currency TYPE TABLE OF zdb_currency.

    lt_currency = VALUE #(
      ( cuky = 'EUR' )
      ( cuky = 'USD' )
      ( cuky = 'INR' )
    ).

    DATA: lt_partner TYPE TABLE OF zdb_partner.

    lt_partner = VALUE #(
      ( partner = '1000011111' name = 'New World IT' type = 'C' )
      ( partner = '1000011112' name = 'North IT'     type = 'C' )
      ( partner = '1000011113' name = 'Global IT'    type = 'K' )
      ( partner = '1000011114' name = 'Central IT'   type = 'K' )
    ).

    DATA: lt_product TYPE TABLE OF zdb_product.

    lt_product = VALUE #(
      ( prod = 'Cloud_Package' descr = 'Cloud Package' price = '100' cuky = 'INR' )
      ( prod = 'Cloud_Office'  descr = 'Cloud Office'  price = '99'  cuky = 'EUR' )
      ( prod = 'Cloud_Trail'   descr = 'Cloud Trial'   price = '100' cuky = 'USD' )
    ).

    DATA: lt_status TYPE TABLE OF zdb_status.

    lt_status = VALUE #(
      ( status = 'O' text = 'Open' )
      ( status = 'I' text = 'InProcess' )
      ( status = 'C' text = 'Completed' )
      ( status = 'R' text = 'Rejected' )
      ( status = 'B' text = 'Blocked' )
    ).

    "Clear existing data
    DELETE FROM zdb_currency.
    DELETE FROM zdb_partner.
    DELETE FROM zdb_product.
    DELETE FROM zdb_status.

    "Insert fresh test data
    INSERT zdb_currency FROM TABLE @LT_currency.
    INSERT zdb_partner  FROM TABLE @LT_partner.
    INSERT zdb_product  FROM TABLE @LT_product.
    INSERT zdb_status   FROM TABLE @LT_status.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.
CLASS lhc_SalesHeader DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.

    METHODS get_instance_authorizations FOR INSTANCE AUTHORIZATION
      IMPORTING keys REQUEST requested_authorizations FOR SalesHeader RESULT result.

    METHODS GenerateOrderNumber FOR DETERMINE ON SAVE
      IMPORTING keys FOR SalesHeader~GenerateOrderNumber.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lhc_SalesHeader IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD get_instance_authorizations.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD GenerateOrderNumber.

    READ ENTITIES OF zi_so_hdr IN LOCAL MODE ENTITY SalesHeader
    FIELDS ( Sorder ) WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    RESULT DATA(lt_sorder).

    DELETE lt_sorder WHERE sorder IS NOT INITIAL.
    IF lt_sorder IS NOT INITIAL.

      SELECT SINGLE FROM zdb_so_hdr FIELDS MAX( sorder ) INTO @DATA(lv_sorder_max).

      MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_so_hdr IN LOCAL MODE ENTITY SalesHeader
      UPDATE FIELDS ( Sorder )
      WITH VALUE #( FOR Ls_sorder IN lt_sorder INDEX INTO i ( %key = ls_sorder-%key
      Sorder = lv_sorder_max + i ) )

      REPORTED DATA(lt_reported).

    ENDIF.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

5. Service Definition

@EndUserText.label: 'Sales Order'
define service ZSD_SalesORDER {
  expose ZC_SO_HDR as SalesOrder;
}

6. Service Binding

Conclusion

Collaborative Draft in SAP RAP Managed Scenario enables multiple users to work efficiently on the same business document without data conflicts.
Using Sales Order as an example, we saw how RAP allows draft creation, collaboration, and activation using CDS views and behavior definitions.

entry.title: RAP New Feature : Generating Managed RAP Applications With Table Entity
entry.content:
Traditionally, when developing a managed RAP application, we create multiple artifacts such as CDS view entities, behavior definitions, service definitions, and service bindings. However, with the introduction of Table Entity, SAP allows us to directly expose database tables in a RAP-compliant way, significantly reducing boilerplate development effort.

This feature is especially useful when:

  • You want to quickly generate a managed RAP application

  • The table structure already satisfies your business requirements

  • You want to minimize intermediate CDS modeling layers

  • You are building internal or utility applications with simple transactional needs

    In this blog, we will explore:

    • What a Table Entity is in RAP

    • How it differs from traditional CDS view entities

    • Step-by-step creation of a Managed RAP application using Table Entity

    • Service exposure and preview in Fiori Elements

      Table Entity:

      • Read and write access is possible using ABAP SQL SELECT, UPDATE, MODIFY, INSERT, and DELETE.

      • A table entity can be used as a data type in ABAP.

        Properties of Table Entities:

        Key Fields – You can define one or more elements as the primary key using the keyword KEY. However, unlike database tables, key fields are optional and it is also possible to have a table entity without a primary key.

        Note: The access pattern of a table entity without a primary key is that it is always read completely, which can result in longer processing times. Single record access is not provided.

        Client Dependency

        Client handling is defined using the header annotation @ClientHandling.type. The possible annotation values are #CLIENT_DEPENDENT or #CLIENT_INDEPENDENT. 

        Flag for Null Values

        A null value on the database is an undefined value. Columns of table entities are by default created as NOT NULL on the SAP HANA database. It is possible to override this default behavior by using the optional addition NULL for a column. 

        Delivery Class

        The delivery class of a table entity controls the transport of table data in installations, upgrades, or client copies, and in transports between customer systems. Annotation  @AbapCatalog.deliveryClass is used to specify delivery class.

         

        • select table entity template.
@ClientHandling.type: #CLIENT_DEPENDENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass: #APPLICATION_DATA
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'travel information'
define table entity ztb_travel
{
  key travel_id : /dmo/travel_id;
  description   : /dmo/description;
  status        : /dmo/travel_status;
    lastchangedat : timestampl;
 

​

 

  • create report/class to insert entries.

 

select travel_id, description, status,lastchangedat FROM /dmo/travel into TABLE (lt_travel).
if sy-subrc eq 0.
insert ztb_travel FROM TABLE _TRAVEL.
if sy-subrc eq 0.
out->write( 'travel data inserted successfully' ).
else.
out->write( 'failed to insert' ).
ENDIF.
endif.



  • Lets make our table entity as root entity so that will create rap application as well

     
@ClientHandling.type: #CLIENT_DEPENDENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass: #APPLICATION_DATA
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'travel information'
define root table entity ztb_travel
{
  key travel_id : /dmo/travel_id;
  description   : /dmo/description;
  status        : /dmo/travel_status;
    lastchangedat : timestampl;    
}

 

Projection View:

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'consumption view for travel table entity'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define root view entity zcb_travel as projection on ztb_travel
{

@UI.facet: [{ label: 'Travel Info', type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE }]
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
@UI.identification: [{ position: 10 }]
    key travel_id,
    .lineItem: [{ position: 20 }]
@UI.identification: [{ position: 20 }]
    
    description,
    .lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]
@UI.identification: [{ position: 30 }]
    
    status
}

 

Interface Behavior Definition:

managed implementation in class zbp_tb_travel unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;
define behavior for ztb_travel alias tb_travel
lock master total etag lastchangedat
draft table zdtbtrav
authorization master ( instance )
{
  create ( authorization : global );
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly ) lastchangedat;
  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Discard;
  draft action Edit;
  draft action Resume;
  draft determine action Prepare;
}


Projection Behavior Definition:

projection implementation in class zbp_cb_travel unique;
strict ( 2 );
use draft;

define behavior for zcb_travel alias tb_travel
{
  use create;
  use update;
  use delete;

  use action Activate;
  use action Discard;
  use action Edit;
  use action Resume;
  use action Prepare;
}


Service Definition:

 

@EndUserText.label: 'sd for table entity travel'
define service Zsd_tb_trav {
  expose zcb_travel as TableEntityTravel;
}

 
Service Binding:

 

generate service binding and test,we generate managed rap application, CRUD operations will be handled by framework itself.

Conclusion:
the table entity in RAP provides a simple and effective way to expose database tables directly in RAP-based applications. It helps developers quickly build transactional services with minimal configuration while still benefiting from the RAP framework’s capabilities. By using table entities, developers can accelerate development and maintain consistency within modern SAP application architecture.
ABAP Connectivity  ABAP Extensibility  SAP BTP ABAP environment ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model

entry.title: Oops we did it again! A brand new top-level ABAP keyword in ABAP CE 2602.
entry.content:
Introduction

New ABAP commands arise only once in most people's lifetimes. But after the ABAP command WITH in AS ABAP 7.51, we have now introduced the new ABAP command MERGE.
The fact that both keywords concern databases is no coincidence. Leveraging the full power of the HANA database is one of ABAP's highest priorities.
Note that we use "command" as a synonym for top-level keywords in this article.

Introducing a new ABAP top-level keyword in the 21st century

What is the problem with introducing a new ABAP keyword? Macros. Consider the following coding:

DEFINE merge.
  WRITE : / &1.
END-OF-DEFINITION. 
 
MERGE 'hello'.

 

After introducing MERGE as a new keyword, this code would have syntax errors. This is why, for example, RAP avoided introducing any new top-level keyword with EML. MODIFY, READ, COMMIT all existed well before RAP. 
But the developers of ABAP SQL are not afraid, as they have already shown in the past. With MERGE, they introduced the concept of a weak ABAP keyword! Macros take precedence. Here is what happens when you use a macro named MERGE in 2602:

So despite a number of warnings, nothing bad happens. You cannot use the new ABAP command MERGE in this program, though. There is and will be no escape mechanism. 

Of course, macros are not available in ABAP Cloud and this is only relevant for customers when MERGE makes it into the next On Premise release.

What does MERGE do?

MERGE is a standard SQL database command. It exists on the HANA database and most other commercial databases. Practically it is a more powerful version of UPDATE, DELETE, INSERT and MODIFY all in one command.

As MERGE is an SQL standard keyword and ABAP is a superset of SQL by tradition, we also opted to keep the command name and thus introduce a new ABAP command. 

Conceptually, MERGE is a very easy operation to understand. Here is the flow:

Here, "when no join partner found" means that the joined table (e.g., internal table) contains a set not contained in the database table. The other direction is not covered yet (see below).

 

A practical example

In this section, we give a very easy example of the MERGE command. Say you have a database table of users, and from time to time, you update it from an Excel spreadsheet stored in an internal table.  

A simple merge command would look like follows:

MERGE INTO users
  USING @Excel AS excel
  ON users~userid = excel~userid
  WHEN MATCHED AND users~managed_by_excel = @abap_true THEN UPDATE SET user_data = excel~user_data
  WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT ALL.

 This command is really self-explanatory. When you want to change the fields inserted into the database, you can also specify them as expressions in the VALUES ( .... ) clause. The usage of DELETE is fairly similar.

Some databases (for example SQL Server) have an additional clause WHEN NOT MATCHED  BY SOURCE, which is triggered when an entry is found in the joined table (e.g. excel) but not in the target table (e.g. users).  This is neither present in ABAP nor on the HANA database yet. 
Thus, conceptually, the join equals a RIGHT OUTER JOIN.

Summary

This article introduces the new ABAP command MERGE, a powerful tool for data manipulation. Due to its ability to join an internal or database table (or a global temporary table for that matter) it can be a very powerful tool in the hands of an ABAP developer.

You may find additional information in the SAP help article here.

entry.title: How to create a RAP application using a parameterized CDS view
entry.content:
Hello everyone, recently I came across a somewhat unusual requirement where I needed to use a parameter in a RAP application. As we know, this isn’t natively supported with Fiori Elements, so I had to look for a way to make it work. After some research and a few tests, I ended up with a solution that I consider quite innovative: using a Fiori Freestyle approach.

The best part is that it requires only a minimal amount of code, something that any ABAP developer can implement without much effort, and it becomes extremely useful whenever you need to work with parameters that are not directly linked to the data exposed by the CDS view.

The idea behind this solution is simple:

Since projection views in RAP do not support CDS parameters directly, we expose a parameterized interface CDS view through a service definition and service binding.

Then, using a Fiori Freestyle application with a Smart Table, we dynamically inject the parameter into the table binding path before the data request is sent to the backend.

This allows the RAP service to consume the parameterized CDS view without requiring complex workarounds.

 

Let’s go through the required steps:

 

1)  Since it’s not possible to add WITH PARAMETERS to a projection view, we need to create an interface CDS view and use it as our “projection.”

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: '<your cds description>'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: false
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true

define view entity <your_parameterized_interface_cds>
  with parameters
    p_date : abap.char(8)

as select from <your_source_cds_or_table>
(
  p_date : $parameters.p_date
)
{
    key <your_key_field>,
        <your_field_1>,
        <your_field_2>,
        <your_field_3>
}

2) Create our service definition normally

3) Create Service Binding ( Odata V2 )

4) Now we have to create the freestyle app with the fiori generator application

5) Now just connect to the S4 and select the service binding created earlier

At this point, you can see the project structure in the Explorer panel on the left. We’ll focus only on the view and controller folders.

 

 

 

6)

First, we need to create our Smart Table in the view folder. Inside the SmartFilterBar, we must set the entitySet from our service binding. After that, we add a customControl in my case, I used a DatePicker, but you can use any control that fits your requirement. I also attached an onDateChange event to the DatePicker so we can capture and manipulate the selected date whenever it changes.

Within the Smart Table, we also need to define the same entitySet. Another very important point here is the beforeRebindTable event. This is the hook that allows us to pass the parameter to the CDS view. It will trigger the onBeforeRebindTable method in the controller, where we can dynamically inject the parameter into the binding before the data is requested.

 

<mvc:View
    xmlns:table="sap.ui.table"
    xmlns="sap.ui.comp.smartfilterbar"
    xmlns:f="sap.f"
    xmlns:m="sap.m"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns:smartTable="sap.ui.comp.smarttable"
    controllerName="<your.app.namespace>.controller.Main"
    height="100%">

<f:DynamicPage
    id="dynamicPage"
    fitContent="true"
    title="{i18n>appTitle}">

<f:header>
<f:DynamicPageHeader>

<SmartFilterBar
    id="smartFilterBar"
    entitySet="<your_entity_set>">

    <controlConfiguration>

        <ControlConfiguration
            key="<your_parameter_field>"
            visibleInAdvancedArea="true"
            label="<your_filter_label>"
            mandatory="true">

            <customControl>

                <m:DatePicker
                    id="datePicker"
                    displayFormat="dd/MM/yyyy"
                    valueFormat="yyyyMMdd"
                    placeholder="dd/mm/yyyy"
                    width="100%"
                    required="true"
                    change="onDateChange"/>

            </customControl>

        </ControlConfiguration>

    </controlConfiguration>

</SmartFilterBar>

</f:DynamicPageHeader>
</f:header>

<f:content>

<smartTable:SmartTable
    id="smartTable"
    beforeRebindTable="onBeforeRebindTable"
    showRowCount="true"
    enableAutoBinding="false"
    entitySet="<your_entity_set>"
    smartFilterId="smartFilterBar"
    useVariantManagement="true"
    tableType="Table"
    initiallyVisibleFields="<your_fields>"
    enableAutoColumnWidth="true"
    showTablePersonalisation="true">

    <smartTable:customToolbar>

        <m:OverflowToolbar design="Transparent">
            <m:ToolbarSpacer/>
        </m:OverflowToolbar>

    </smartTable:customToolbar>

</smartTable:SmartTable>

</f:content>

</f:DynamicPage>

</mvc:View>

7) In this step, we implement the logic inside the onBeforeRebindTable method, which is responsible for dynamically passing the parameter to the CDS view before the Smart Table triggers the data request.

First, the code retrieves the value entered in the DatePicker using byId. This value will be used as the parameter for the CDS. A local variable (sDate) is initialized to store the selected date.

Next, a validation is performed to ensure that the date field is filled in. Since this parameter is mandatory for the query, if no value is provided the DatePicker is set to the Error state and a validation message is displayed. At the same time, the table binding is stopped using:

oBindingParams.preventTableBind = true;

This prevents the Smart Table from calling the backend without the required parameter, avoiding unnecessary requests and possible errors.

If the date is correctly filled in, any previous error state is cleared by setting the ValueState back to None.

Finally, the most important part happens: the table binding path is updated dynamically. Here we pass the selected date to the parameterized CDS by changing the binding path of the Smart Table:

setTableBindingPath("/<EntitySet>(p_date='${sDate}')/Set")

This is what allows the RAP application to consume a CDS with parameters, because the parameter is injected at runtime just before the data is requested from the backend.

onBeforeRebindTable: function (oEvent) {

    let oDatePicker = this.byId("datePicker");
    let sDate = "";

    if (oDatePicker) {
        sDate = oDatePicker.getValue();
    }

    if (!sDate) {

        if (oDatePicker) {
            oDatePicker.setValueState("Error");
            oDatePicker.setValueStateText("Please inform the date.");
        }

        let oBindingParams = oEvent.getParameter("bindingParams");

        if (oBindingParams) {
            oBindingParams.preventTableBind = true;
        }

        return;
    }

    if (oDatePicker) {
        oDatePicker.setValueState("None");
    }

    this.getView()
        .byId("smartTable")
        .setTableBindingPath("/<your_entity_set>(p_date='" + sDate + "')/Set");

}

And Vooooalá!

Conclusion

Although RAP and Fiori Elements provide powerful tools for building enterprise applications, there are still scenarios where certain features are not directly supported, such as consuming parameterized CDS views in projection layers.

By combining RAP services with a lightweight Fiori Freestyle approach, we can overcome this limitation in a simple and flexible way. Using events like beforeRebindTable and dynamically modifying the binding path allows parameters to be passed to the CDS view at runtime.

This approach requires only a small amount of additional UI code and can be implemented quickly by ABAP developers who are already familiar with RAP and Fiori.

I hope this solution helps others facing similar challenges when working with parameterized CDS views in RAP applications.

entry.title: Visual Filters in SAP Fiori Elements: A Complete Guide
entry.content:
Introduction :  SAP Fiori Elements provides several smart UI features that help users interact with business data more efficiently. One such feature is the Visual Filter, which offers a graphical way to filter data in applications like the Analytical List Page. Instead of entering filter values manually, users can select values directly from charts or graphical elements, making the filtering process faster and more intuitive. Visual Filters enhance the user experience by combining data visualization with filtering capabilities, allowing users to quickly analyze data and refine their search results.

Visual Filter:

A Visual Filter in SAP Fiori Elements is a graphical filter control used in the Filter Bar of applications like  Analytical List Page.
Instead of typing filter values manually, users can select values directly from a chart (bar, line, or donut) to filter the data.

A Visual Filter is a small interactive chart in the filter bar that allows users to filter data visually.

-----> Visual Filter = Chart + Filter

It shows aggregated data in a small chart, and when the user clicks a value, the application filters the table data automatically.

Example:

Month_YearNet Value

MAY-20231930.7
SEP-2022254.6
FEB-2023245.5

When a user clicks FEB-2023 (245.5) in the Visual Filter, the application automatically filters the data and displays the corresponding results in the chart and list. Please  find the below Screen shot for your Reference.

 

How to Create Visual Filter  in  BTP BAS:

  1. Open SAP Business Application Studio.

  2. Go to the Explorer panel.

  3. Locate your Fiori project (for example: analytical_report).

  4. Right-click on the project.

  5. Select Open Guided Development.

This option helps developers configure Fiori Elements features

After opening Guided Development:

  1. Select Project Guides.

  2. Choose Analytical List Page.

  3. Scroll down in the options.

  4. Click Add a New Visual Filter to the Filter Bar.

This option is used to create a visual filter chart in the filter bar.

After selecting Add a New Visual Filter to the Filter Bar, a configuration wizard will open.

You will see these tabs:

  • About – Information about the visual filter feature

  • Step 1 – Select entity set and dimension

  • Step 2 – Select measure and chart type

  • Step 3 – Generate the annotation and save

Once the configuration is completed, BAS automatically generates the required CDS/UI annotations.

Please find the Below Screen shot for Your Reference:

Step1:

  <Annotation Term="UI.Chart" Qualifier="Step1_CQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.ChartDefinitionType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="ChartType" EnumMember="UI.ChartType/Bar"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Title" String="Purchase Order Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Description" String="Purchase Order Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Dimensions">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Month_Year</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="DimensionAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartDimensionAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Dimension" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartDimensionRoleType/Category"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Measures">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Amount</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="MeasureAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartMeasureAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Measure" PropertyPath="Amount"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartMeasureRoleType/Axis1"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>

Step2:

 <Annotation Term="UI.PresentationVariant" Qualifier="Step2_pQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.PresentationVariantType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="Visualizations">
                            <Collection>
                                <AnnotationPath>@UI.Chart#Step1_CQ</AnnotationPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>

Step3:

 <Annotation Term="Common.ValueList">
                    <Record Type="Common.ValueListType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="CollectionPath" String="Ztest_ALP_Report"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="PresentationVariantQualifier" String="Step2_pQ"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Parameters">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="Common.ValueListParameterInOut">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="LocalDataProperty" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="ValueListProperty" String="Month_Year"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>

After completing all the steps, the Visual Filter is successfully added to the Analytical List Page. The filter now appears in the filter bar as an interactive chart, allowing users to select values and automatically filter the data displayed in the charts and table.

 Before  add Visual Filter --- > in the Annotation.xml File.

<edmx:Edmx xmlns:edmx="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edmx" Version="4.0">
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Common.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1" Alias="Common"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/UI.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1" Alias="UI"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV/$metadata">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV" Alias="SAP"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:DataServices>
        <Schema xmlns="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edm" Namespace="local">
           
        </Schema>
    </edmx:DataServices>
</edmx:Edmx>

 After  add Visual Filter --- > in the Annotation.xml File.

  <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_ALP_ReportType">
                <Annotation Term="UI.Chart" Qualifier="Step1_CQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.ChartDefinitionType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="ChartType" EnumMember="UI.ChartType/Bar"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Title" String="Purchase Order Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Description" String="Purchase Order Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Dimensions">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Month_Year</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="DimensionAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartDimensionAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Dimension" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartDimensionRoleType/Category"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Measures">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Amount</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="MeasureAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartMeasureAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Measure" PropertyPath="Amount"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartMeasureRoleType/Axis1"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
                <Annotation Term="UI.PresentationVariant" Qualifier="Step2_pQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.PresentationVariantType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="Visualizations">
                            <Collection>
                                <AnnotationPath>@UI.Chart#Step1_CQ</AnnotationPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
            <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_ALP_ReportType/Month_Year">
                <Annotation Term="Common.ValueList">
                    <Record Type="Common.ValueListType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="CollectionPath" String="Ztest_ALP_Report"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="PresentationVariantQualifier" String="Step2_pQ"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Parameters">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="Common.ValueListParameterInOut">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="LocalDataProperty" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="ValueListProperty" String="Month_Year"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>

Note: Instead of following the guided development steps, you can also add the required annotations directly in the annotation file based on the client’s requirements. This approach allows developers to configure Visual Filters manually using CDS/UI annotations.

Onces done  all step sucessfuly you wull see visula filer in the  anaytical list page

Complete  Code --> Visula Filter + Analytical List Page

1.CDS View:
---------------
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZALPR'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'ALP Report'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define view Ztest_ALP_Report
  as select from ztest_sales_tab
{
  key vbeln as SalesDocument,
      vkorg as SalesOrg,
      werks as Plant,
      vtweg as DistrChannel,
      @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
      netwr as Amount,
      waerk as Currency,
      audat as D_Date,
      case substring( audat, 5, 2 )
      when '01' then concat( 'JAN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '02' then concat( 'FEB-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '03' then concat( 'MAR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '04' then concat( 'APR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '05' then concat( 'MAY-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '06' then concat( 'JUN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '07' then concat( 'JUL-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '08' then concat( 'AUG-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '09' then concat( 'SEP-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '10' then concat( 'OCT-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '11' then concat( 'NOV-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '12' then concat( 'DEC-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )

      end   as Month_Year
}
2.Meta data extension View:
---------------------------
@Metadata.layer: #CORE

@UI.chart: [{
    qualifier: 'chartDefault',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #PIE,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    }
   ]

@UI.presentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'Default',
    visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART ,qualifier: 'chartDefault'}]
   
     
}]



annotate entity Ztest_ALP_Report with
{
  .lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]

  SalesDocument;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 20 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 20 }]
  SalesOrg;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]
  Plant;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 40 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 40 }]
  DistrChannel;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 50 }]
  .dataPoint.qualifier: 'Amount'
  Amount;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 60 }]
  Currency;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 70 }]
  D_Date;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 80 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 80 }]
  Month_Year;

}

3. Annotation.xml File:
-----------------------
<edmx:Edmx xmlns:edmx="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edmx" Version="4.0">
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Common.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1" Alias="Common"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/UI.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1" Alias="UI"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV/$metadata">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV" Alias="SAP"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:DataServices>
        <Schema xmlns="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edm" Namespace="local">
           
            <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_ALP_ReportType">
                <Annotation Term="UI.Chart" Qualifier="Step1_CQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.ChartDefinitionType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="ChartType" EnumMember="UI.ChartType/Bar"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Title" String="Purchase Order Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Description" String="Purchase Order Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Dimensions">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Month_Year</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="DimensionAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartDimensionAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Dimension" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartDimensionRoleType/Category"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Measures">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Amount</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="MeasureAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartMeasureAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Measure" PropertyPath="Amount"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartMeasureRoleType/Axis1"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
                <Annotation Term="UI.PresentationVariant" Qualifier="Step2_pQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.PresentationVariantType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="Visualizations">
                            <Collection>
                                <AnnotationPath>@UI.Chart#Step1_CQ</AnnotationPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
            <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_ALP_ReportType/Month_Year">
                <Annotation Term="Common.ValueList">
                    <Record Type="Common.ValueListType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="CollectionPath" String="Ztest_ALP_Report"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="PresentationVariantQualifier" String="Step2_pQ"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Parameters">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="Common.ValueListParameterInOut">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="LocalDataProperty" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="ValueListProperty" String="Month_Year"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
           
           
        </Schema>
    </edmx:DataServices>
</edmx:Edmx>


Conclusion:  In conclusion, Visual Filters in SAP Fiori Elements provide an intuitive way to filter and analyze data using graphical charts. They help users quickly understand data trends and apply filters easily, improving both usability and efficiency.

 

Thank You,

B Naresh Kumar Nayak

entry.title: SAP Fiori Elements Full Tutorial in SAP BTP BAS – End-to-End Guide
entry.content:
Introduction: SAP Fiori Elements is a framework that enables developers to build SAP Fiori applications quickly using predefined templates and annotations. Instead of writing large amounts of UI code, developers can define application behavior and UI structure using CDS views and OData services. SAP Fiori Elements automatically generates the user interface based on these annotations, ensuring consistency with SAP Fiori design guidelines.

What is SAP Fiori Elements?

SAP Fiori Elements is a metadata-driven UI framework built on SAPUI5 that automatically generates enterprise-grade SAP Fiori applications .SAP Fiori Elements provides standardized floorplans that cover the majority of enterprise application scenarios.

One Word Definition (Fiori Element)--> SAP Fiori Elements can be described as a standardized floorplan-based application framework.

Fiori Elements---> ( CDS+ Annotation+ Odata services ) .

Types of Fiori Elemnts:
1. List Report – Displays and filters business data in tabular format
2.Object Page – Shows detailed information about a single business object.
3.Analytical List Page (ALP) – KPIs + charts + table
4.Overview Page (OVP) – Dashboard-style layout with multiple cards.
5.Worklist– Displays a list of items requiring action.

Mandatory Components : To develop and run a Fiori Elements application in SAP BTP that consumes an On-Premise SAP system, the following components are mandatory
1. SAP Cloud Connector.
2. SAP Business Application Studio.
3. SAP BTP Trial Account.
4. On-Premise System Details (For SAP S/4HANA or ECC Systems ).

 

Topic: Complete SAP Fiori Elements Learning Series: I have developed and covered all major concepts related to SAP Fiori Elements and BTP integration.

Please go through the link below to learn how to create SAP Fiori Elements applications. The step-by-step process for developing Fiori Elements apps is explained in detail in the following tutorial.

1.How to Connect On-Premise System to BTP BAS : https://community.sap.com/t5/abap-blog-posts/how-to-connect-an-on-premise-abap-system-to-sap-btp-bas-using-sap-cloud/ba-p/14340330 

2.List Reort:  https://community.sap.com/t5/abap-blog-posts/how-to-create-a-list-report-in-sap-fiori-elements-step-by-step-guide/ba-p/14346960 

3.Object Page:  https://community.sap.com/t5/abap-blog-posts/how-to-create-an-object-list-page-in-sap-fiori-elements-step-by-step-guide/ba-p/14347253 

4.Analytical List Page (ALP): https://community.sap.com/t5/abap-blog-posts/how-to-create-an-analytical-list-page-in-sap-fiori-elements-step-by-step/ba-p/14348144 

5.Overview Page (OVP): https://community.sap.com/t5/abap-blog-posts/overview-page-in-sap-fiori-elements-a-step-by-step-guide/ba-p/14348973 

 

Conclusion : In conclusion, SAP Fiori Elements simplifies the development of SAP Fiori applications by using templates and metadata-driven approaches. It reduces development effort, ensures a consistent user experience, and allows developers to focus more on business logic rather than UI coding.

Thank  You,

B Naresh Kumar Nayak

entry.title: RAP Unmanaged Draft: Early Numbering and Associations for Unique IDs
entry.content:
INTRODUCTION

In RAP, Implementing numbering mechanisms during these operations is essential to ensure unique identifiers for newly created entities.
This blog is exposure the best for numbering during Creation and Creation by Association, in Unmanaged Scenario with Draft. How to handle number creation with Draft Scenario's.

Some Information about Numbering
Basically, Numbering is the concept to process assigning unique values to the key fields of business entities during Create and Create By Association Operations.

Unmanaged Early Numbering:

  • Developers need to implement custom code logic to create and assign keys values during the creation and create by association operation. 
  • %is_draft – It is a component of the transactional key (%tky) in RAP. 
    • It tells the framework whether to operate on the draft table or the active persistent table for a given operation. 
    • %is_draft = mk-on reads from draft table 
    • %is_draft = mk-off reads from active table 

Problem Statement

A sales clerk begins creating a Sales Order in Fiori. The order header is saved as a draft, but items need to be added immediately under that draft. To ensure consistency, the system must generate a unique Sales Order ID right away and assign incremental item numbers without waiting for activation.

Importance:

  • Early numbering guarantees unique identification across drafts and active records.

  • Associations ensure header–item linkage is preserved.

  • Draft handling allows users to save incomplete transactions safely and activate later without losing data integrity.

Behavior Definition of Root Entity

early numbering → enables key generation during root creation.

unmanaged implementation in class zbp_i_vd_head unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;

define behavior for ZI_VD_HEAD alias Header
draft table zdft_vd_head
lock master
total etag CreatedOn
authorization master ( instance )
early numbering // Early numbering declaration in root entity
{
  create;
  update;
  delete;
  association _item { create; }
  field ( readonly : update ) CustNo;

  draft action Edit;
  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Resume;
  draft action Discard;
  draft determine action Prepare;
}
mapping for zvd_t_hsale {
  CustNo     = cust_no;
  SalesOrder = sales_order;
  CreatedOn  = created_on;
  EntryTime  = entry_time;
  Name       = name;
  DocCat     = doc_cat;
}


Behavior Definition of Item Entity

early numbering → enables key generation during item creation.

define behavior for ZI_VD_ITEM alias Item
draft table zdft_vd_item
lock dependent by _head
authorization dependent by _head
early numbering // Early numbering declaration in item entity
{
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly ) CustNo;
  association _head;

  mapping for zvd_t_isale {
    CustNo     = cust_no;
    SalesOrder = sales_order;
    DocItem    = doc_item;
    MatNumber  = mat_number;
    MatGroup   = mat_group;
    CreatedOn  = created_on;
  }
}

Behavior Pool Implementation

This method ensures that every new entity gets a unique identifier (CustNo) during creation if it wasn’t already provided.

UUID Generation:

  • cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_x16_static( ) generates a globally unique key.

  • This avoids collisions and guarantees uniqueness across drafts and active records.

Mapped Root:

  • %cid → correlates client-side temporary IDs with backend entities.

  • %key → holds the generated or existing key.

  • %is_draft → ensures draft state is preserved.

  • CustNo → the actual business key stored in the entity.

METHOD earlynumbering_create.
  LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(entity) WHERE CustNo IS INITIAL.
    DATA(ls_uuid) = cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_x16_static( ).

    APPEND VALUE #( %cid      = entity-%cid
                    %key      = VALUE #( CustNo = ls_uuid )
                    %is_draft = entity-%is_draft
                    CustNo    = ls_uuid ) TO mapped-header.
  ENDLOOP.

  LOOP AT entities INTO entity WHERE CustNo IS NOT INITIAL.
    APPEND VALUE #( %cid      = entity-%cid
                    %key      = entity-%key
                    %is_draft = entity-%is_draft ) TO mapped-header.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

Mapped Item:

This method handles early numbering when creating items via association (CREATE-BY-ASSOCIATION).

  • First, it retrieves the maximum cust_no from the database table zvd_t_isale for the current entity.

  • Then, it checks existing items in the helper class buffer (gt_isale) to ensure numbering continuity.

  • For each new item (DocItem IS INITIAL), it increments the maximum by 10 and assigns the new number.

  • %cid → client-side correlation ID.

  • %key → generated key with CustNo.

  • %is_draft → preserves draft state.

  • CustNo → ensures linkage to the root entity.

METHOD earlynumbering_cba_item.
  LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(entity).
    SELECT MAX( cust_no ) FROM zvd_t_isale
      INTO (lv_max)
      WHERE cust_no = -CustNo.

    LOOP AT zcl_vd_helper=>get_instance( )->gt_isale INTO DATA(item)
      WHERE cust_no = entity-%key-CustNo.
      IF item-doc_item > lv_max.
        lv_max = item-doc_item.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.

    LOOP AT entity-%target INTO DATA(t_item) WHERE DocItem IS INITIAL.
      lv_max += 10.

      APPEND VALUE #( %cid      = t_item-%cid
                      %key      = VALUE #( CustNo = entity-%key-CustNo )
                      %is_draft = t_item-%is_draft
                      CustNo    = entity-%key-CustNo ) TO mapped-item.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

  • Item inherits lock and authorization from the header.

  • Early numbering ensures item keys are generated consistently.

  • Association links items back to the header BO.

Persistence Methods

  • Create Method
METHOD create.
  LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(entity).
    DATA(header) = CORRESPONDING zvd_t_hsale( entity MAPPING FROM ENTITY ).

    header-created_on = sy-datum.
    header-entry_time = sy-datum.
    header-name       = sy-uname.

    APPEND header TO gt_hsale.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.
  • Create-By-Association Method
METHOD cba.
  LOOP AT entities_cba INTO DATA(entity_cba).
    LOOP AT entity_cba-%target INTO DATA(target_item).
      DATA(item) = CORRESPONDING zvd_t_isale( target_item MAPPING FROM ENTITY ).

      item-cust_no   = entity_cba-%key-CustNo.
      item-created_on = sy-datum.

      APPEND item TO gt_isale.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

Result

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Conclusion

By combining Draft Handling, Associations, and Early Numbering, RAP unmanaged scenarios can support complex business objects like Sales Orders, Purchase Orders. This approach ensures flexibility, control, and consistency in transactional applications.

Thank You...

entry.title: How to Create an Object List Page in SAP Fiori Elements – Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
Introduction : SAP Fiori Elements provides predefined floorplans that help developers build enterprise applications quickly with minimal coding. One of the most commonly used floorplans is the Object Page. The Object Page is designed to display detailed information about a single business object in a structured and organized layout. It allows users to view, edit, and manage detailed data while navigating through different sections of related information within the same page.

SAP Fiori Elements Object Page :

Object Page =  A detailed screen that shows complete information about a selected business object.

When a user selects one record from a List Report, the system navigates to the Object Page to show the full details.

Flow of  Analytical List Page:

  1. CDS View
  2. Annotation
  3. Odata service
  4. SAP Fiori Elements ( Analytical List Page) Template
  5. SAP Fiori Application UI

Step 1: Create CDS View and Metadata Extension:

First, create the Core Data Services (CDS) view that exposes the required business data.
After creating the CDS view, create a Metadata Extension and add the necessary UI annotations to define how the data should appear on the SAP Fiori Elements Object Page.

The metadata extension is used to separate the UI annotations from the main CDS view, which helps maintain a cleaner and more structured development approach.

Note: It is assumed that you already know how to create a CDS View and a Metadata Extension in the ABAP environment. Therefore, this article does not cover the step-by-step creation process. Instead, the examples below directly demonstrate the CDS view and Metadata Extension with the required annotations used for the SAP Fiori Elements Object Page.

1. CDS View:

Code:

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZOLR'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Object List Report'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define view ZCDS_ObjectList_Report
  as select from ztest_sales_tab
{
  key vbeln as SalesDocument,

      vkorg as SalesOrg,
      werks as Plant,
      vtweg as DistrChannel,
      @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
      netwr as Amount,
      waerk as Currency,
      audat as CreationDate,
      case substring( audat, 5, 2 )
      when '01' then concat( 'JAN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '02' then concat( 'FEB-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '03' then concat( 'MAR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '04' then concat( 'APR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '05' then concat( 'MAY-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '06' then concat( 'JUN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '07' then concat( 'JUL-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '08' then concat( 'AUG-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '09' then concat( 'SEP-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '10' then concat( 'OCT-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '11' then concat( 'NOV-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '12' then concat( 'DEC-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )

      end   as Month_Year,
       'Purchase Order Title '              as sampleTitle,
      'My Purchase Order for Goods Services' as sampleDes
    
}

2. Meta data Extension:

Code:

@Metadata.layer: #CORE
@Search.searchable: true


@UI.headerInfo: {
 typeName: 'Purchase Order',
 typeNamePlural: 'Purchase OrderS',
 title: {  type: #STANDARD, value: 'sampleTitle' },
 description:{ type: #STANDARD, value: 'sampleDes' }
 //imageUrl: 'image1'

}


annotate view ZCDS_ObjectList_Report with



{


   .facet: [{
          id: 'General',
          purpose: #STANDARD,
          label: 'Purchase Order',
          type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE
      }]
      
  .defaultSearchElement: true
  .lineItem: [{position: 10}]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]
  .identification: [{position: 10  }]
  SalesDocument;
  .lineItem: [{position: 20}]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 20 }]
  .identification: [{position: 20  }]
  SalesOrg;
  .lineItem: [{position: 30}]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 30 }]
  .identification: [{position: 30  }]
  Plant;
  .lineItem: [{position: 40}]
  .identification: [{position: 40  }]
  DistrChannel;
  .lineItem: [{position: 50}]
  .identification: [{position: 50  }]
  .dataPoint: { title: 'Amount' , criticality: 'CAmount'}
  Amount;
  .lineItem: [{position: 60}]
  .identification: [{position: 60  }]
  Currency;
  .lineItem: [{position: 70}]
  .identification: [{position: 70  }]
  CreationDate;

  .lineItem: [{position: 80,label: 'Month Year'}]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 40  }]
  .identification: [{position: 80  }]
  .dataPoint: { title: 'Month & Year' }
  Month_Year;

}

Step 2: Consume the CDS View in an OData Service:

Note: It is assumed that you already know how to consume a CDS View in an OData service. Therefore, this article does not cover the step-by-step process. Instead, the explanation below directly continues with the required configuration and examples.

Go to SEGW  Tcode and open your  Project and  to add your CDS View .

After adding the CDS entity, the Entity Type, Entity Set, and related properties will be generated automatically in the project.

Step 3:  Create SAP Fiori Application using Object Page Template

Follow the steps  :

Open  BAS Tool and  Click on File and Select as New Project from Template  and   chose  List Report Page template .Please provide the necessary details and Finish It.

After completing these steps, the system automatically generates the SAP Fiori Elements Object Page application.

Once all the above steps are completed and the configuration is done, preview the application in SAP Business Application Studio. The application will load, and you will be able to see the Object List Page with the data displayed.

After completing all the configuration steps, preview the application in SAP Business Application Studio. The application opens with the Object List Page, which displays the list of records in a tabular format.

The Object List Page provides the following features:

  • Filter Bar to search data using fields such as:

    • Sales Document

    • Sales Organization

    • Plant

    • Month_Year

  • Table View displaying all the records retrieved from the OData service.

  • Navigation Arrow on each row for accessing detailed information.

Navigation to Object Page :

Each row in the table contains a navigation arrow ( > ) on the right side.

When you click the arrow icon, the application navigates to the Object Page, where detailed information about the selected record is displayed

Example:

List Report → Object Page

5000000001 > opens detailed view

The Object Page shows the complete information of the selected Sales Document.

 

Conclusion: In conclusion, the Object Page in SAP Fiori Elements provides a structured way to display and manage detailed information about a specific business object. By organizing data into sections and enabling actions such as viewing and editing, it improves usability and helps users efficiently work with business data within a single interface.

Thank You,

B Naresh Kumar Nayak

entry.title: KPI (Key Performance Indicator): A Simple Guide
entry.content:
Introduction:  A Key Performance Indicator (KPI) is a measurable value used to evaluate how effectively a business or organization is achieving its key objectives. In SAP Fiori Elements, KPIs are used to display important business metrics in a clear and visual format. They help users quickly monitor performance, identify trends, and make data-driven decisions.

KPI (Key Performance Indicator):  It is a measurable value used to evaluate how effectively a business or organization is achieving its objectives. KPIs help businesses monitor performance, identify trends, and support data-driven decision-making.

In modern enterprise applications built with SAP Fiori Elements, KPIs provide a quick overview of key business metrics directly within dashboards and analytical applications.

KPI in Analytical List Page:

The Analytical List Page (ALP) is a powerful Fiori Elements template that combines:

  • KPIs
  • Charts
  • Filters
  • Tables

KPIs are displayed at the Top of the page and help users quickly analyze business data.

Note: In this article, I have not covered the creation of the CDS View, OData service exposure, or the application creation in SAP BTP, as these topics were already explained in my previous blogs. In this post, I focus only on providing an example of the KPI annotations and explaining where these annotations are added in the project.

CDS:

You can add the KPI annotations in the Metadata Extension file. This approach helps keep UI-related annotations separate from the core CDS view and allows developers to manage UI configurations more efficiently.

KPI Annotation:

.chart: [{
    qualifier: 'chartDefault',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #PIE,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    },
    {
    qualifier: 'kpidata',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #DONUT,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    }

   ]

@UI.presentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'Default',
    visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART ,qualifier: 'chartDefault'}]
    },
 {
 qualifier: 'KPIAMT',text: 'KPI: Amount by Year',
 visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART,qualifier: 'kpidata'},{ type: #AS_DATAPOINT,qualifier: 'Amount'
}]

}]

@UI.selectionVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    text: 'Amount by Year'
}]
@UI.selectionPresentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    presentationVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    selectionVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT'
}]

Once the annotations are added in the Metadata Extension, you can configure the KPI annotation in the manifest.json file so that it is recognized and displayed in the Analytical List Page application.

qualifier: 'KPIAMT'
 

"keyPerformanceIndicators": {
                            "kpiNayak": {
                                "model": "KPI1",
                                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                                "qualifier": "KPIAMT"
                            }
                        }

Path:

sap.ui.generic.app → pages → AnalyticalListPage → component → settings → keyPerformanceIndicators
 "pages": {
            "AnalyticalListPage|Ztest_ALP_Report": {
                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                "component": {
                    "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.AnalyticalListPage",
                    "list": true,
                    "settings": {
                        "condensedTableLayout": true,
                        "showGoButtonOnFilterBar": true,
                        "qualifier": "Default",
                        "autoHide": true,
                        "smartVariantManagement": false,
                        "tableSettings": {
                            "multiSelect": false,
                            "type": "AnalyticalTable"
                        },
                        "keyPerformanceIndicators": {
                            "kpiNayak": {
                                "model": "KPI1",
                                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                                "qualifier": "KPIAMT"
                            }
                        }
                    }
                },

Note : After that, ensure that the model used for the KPI configuration is also created under the models section in the manifest.json file. The same model name defined for the KPI must be maintained in the models configuration so that the application can correctly retrieve the KPI data from the OData service.

The model name used in the KPI configuration (for example, KPI1) must also be created under the models section in the manifest.json file. The same model name should be maintained .

Path:

sap.ui5 → models → KPI1

    "KPI1": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false
                }
            },
"models": {
            "i18n": {
                "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
                "settings": {
                    "bundleName": "analyticalreport.i18n.i18n"
                }
            },
            "": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false,
                    "metadataUrlParams": {
                        "sap-value-list": "none"
                    }
                }
            },
            "KPI1": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false
                }
            },

Once all the configurations are completed, the KPI is successfully created. You can see the KPI displayed in the Analytical List Page as shown in the screenshot below for your reference.

Full Codes: This code example covers the Analytical List Page, including the configuration of Visual Filters and KPI annotations used in the application.

1.CDS View:
-------------
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZALPR'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'ALP Report'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define view Ztest_ALP_Report
  as select from ztest_sales_tab
{
  key vbeln as SalesDocument,
      vkorg as SalesOrg,
      werks as Plant,
      vtweg as DistrChannel,
      @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
      netwr as Amount,
      waerk as Currency,
      audat as D_Date,
      case substring( audat, 5, 2 )
      when '01' then concat( 'JAN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '02' then concat( 'FEB-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '03' then concat( 'MAR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '04' then concat( 'APR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '05' then concat( 'MAY-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '06' then concat( 'JUN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '07' then concat( 'JUL-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '08' then concat( 'AUG-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '09' then concat( 'SEP-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '10' then concat( 'OCT-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '11' then concat( 'NOV-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '12' then concat( 'DEC-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )

      end   as Month_Year
}
___________________________________
2.Meta data Extension:
------------------------
@Metadata.layer: #CORE

@UI.chart: [{
    qualifier: 'chartDefault',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #PIE,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    },
    {
    qualifier: 'kpidata',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #DONUT,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    }

   ]

@UI.presentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'Default',
    visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART ,qualifier: 'chartDefault'}]
    },
 {
 qualifier: 'KPIAMT',text: 'KPI: Amount by Year',
 visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART,qualifier: 'kpidata'},{ type: #AS_DATAPOINT,qualifier: 'Amount'
}]

}]

@UI.selectionVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    text: 'Amount by Year'
}]
@UI.selectionPresentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    presentationVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    selectionVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT'
}]


annotate entity Ztest_ALP_Report with
{
  .lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]

  SalesDocument;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 20 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 20 }]
  SalesOrg;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]
  Plant;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 40 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 40 }]
  DistrChannel;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 50 }]
  .dataPoint.title: 'Total Amount'
  .dataPoint.qualifier: 'Amount'
  Amount;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 60 }]
  Currency;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 70 }]
  D_Date;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 80 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 80 }]
  Month_Year;

}
__________________________________
3. Annotation.xml file:
-----------------------------
<edmx:Edmx xmlns:edmx="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edmx" Version="4.0">
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Common.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1" Alias="Common"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/UI.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1" Alias="UI"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV/$metadata">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV" Alias="SAP"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:DataServices>
        <Schema xmlns="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edm" Namespace="local">
           
            <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_ALP_ReportType">
                <Annotation Term="UI.Chart" Qualifier="Step1_CQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.ChartDefinitionType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="ChartType" EnumMember="UI.ChartType/Bar"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Title" String="Purchase Order Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Description" String="Purchase Order Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Dimensions">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Month_Year</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="DimensionAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartDimensionAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Dimension" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartDimensionRoleType/Category"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Measures">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Amount</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="MeasureAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartMeasureAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Measure" PropertyPath="Amount"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartMeasureRoleType/Axis1"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
                <Annotation Term="UI.PresentationVariant" Qualifier="Step2_pQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.PresentationVariantType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="Visualizations">
                            <Collection>
                                <AnnotationPath>@UI.Chart#Step1_CQ</AnnotationPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
            <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_ALP_ReportType/Month_Year">
                <Annotation Term="Common.ValueList">
                    <Record Type="Common.ValueListType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="CollectionPath" String="Ztest_ALP_Report"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="PresentationVariantQualifier" String="Step2_pQ"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Parameters">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="Common.ValueListParameterInOut">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="LocalDataProperty" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="ValueListProperty" String="Month_Year"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
           
           
        </Schema>
    </edmx:DataServices>
</edmx:Edmx>
 _____________________________          
4. Manifest.json File:
----------------------------------------------
{
    "_version": "1.82.0",
    "sap.app": {
        "id": "analyticalreport",
        "type": "application",
        "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
        "applicationVersion": {
            "version": "0.0.1"
        },
        "title": "{{appTitle}}",
        "description": "{{appDescription}}",
        "resources": "resources.json",
        "sourceTemplate": {
            "id": "@sap/generator-fiori:alp",
            "version": "1.20.4",
            "toolsId": "530787e5-fdb9-4d9d-87bf-ca33db19cf1d"
        },
        "dataSources": {
            "ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_ANNO_MDL": {
                "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_ANNO_MDL',Version='0001')/$value/",
                "type": "ODataAnnotation",
                "settings": {
                    "localUri": "localService/mainService/ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_ANNO_MDL.xml"
                }
            },
            "annotation": {
                "type": "ODataAnnotation",
                "uri": "annotations/annotation.xml",
                "settings": {
                    "localUri": "annotations/annotation.xml"
                }
            },
            "mainService": {
                "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV/",
                "type": "OData",
                "settings": {
                    "annotations": [
                        "ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_ANNO_MDL",
                        "annotation"
                    ],
                    "localUri": "localService/mainService/metadata.xml",
                    "odataVersion": "2.0"
                }
            }
        }
    },
    "sap.ui": {
        "technology": "UI5",
        "icons": {
            "icon": "",
            "favIcon": "",
            "phone": "",
            "phone@2": "",
            "tablet": "",
            "tablet@2": ""
        },
        "deviceTypes": {
            "desktop": true,
            "tablet": true,
            "phone": true
        }
    },
    "sap.ui5": {
        "flexEnabled": true,
        "dependencies": {
            "minUI5Version": "1.145.0",
            "libs": {
                "sap.m": {},
                "sap.ui.core": {},
                "sap.ushell": {},
                "sap.f": {},
                "sap.ui.comp": {},
                "sap.ui.generic.app": {},
                "sap.suite.ui.generic.template": {}
            }
        },
        "contentDensities": {
            "compact": true,
            "cozy": true
        },
        "models": {
            "i18n": {
                "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
                "settings": {
                    "bundleName": "analyticalreport.i18n.i18n"
                }
            },
            "": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false,
                    "metadataUrlParams": {
                        "sap-value-list": "none"
                    }
                }
            },
            "KPI1": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false
                }
            },
            "@i18n": {
                "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
                "uri": "i18n/i18n.properties"
            }
        },
        "resources": {
            "css": []
        },
        "routing": {
            "config": {},
            "routes": [],
            "targets": {}
        }
    },
    "sap.ui.generic.app": {
        "_version": "1.3.0",
        "settings": {
            "forceGlobalRefresh": false,
            "objectPageHeaderType": "Dynamic",
            "considerAnalyticalParameters": true,
            "showDraftToggle": false
        },
        "pages": {
            "AnalyticalListPage|Ztest_ALP_Report": {
                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                "component": {
                    "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.AnalyticalListPage",
                    "list": true,
                    "settings": {
                        "condensedTableLayout": true,
                        "showGoButtonOnFilterBar": true,
                        "qualifier": "Default",
                        "autoHide": true,
                        "smartVariantManagement": false,
                        "tableSettings": {
                            "multiSelect": false,
                            "type": "AnalyticalTable"
                        },
                        "keyPerformanceIndicators": {
                            "kpiNayak": {
                                "model": "KPI1",
                                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                                "qualifier": "KPIAMT"
                            }
                        }
                    }
                },
                "pages": {
                    "ObjectPage|Ztest_ALP_Report": {
                        "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                        "defaultLayoutTypeIfExternalNavigation": "MidColumnFullScreen",
                        "component": {
                            "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ObjectPage"
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    },
    "sap.fiori": {
        "registrationIds": [],
        "archeType": "analytical"
    }
}

 

Conclusion : In conclusion, KPIs in SAP Fiori Elements provide a quick and effective way to monitor critical business metrics. By presenting key values in a visual and easy-to-understand format, KPIs help users track performance and take timely actions to improve business outcomes.

Thank You,

B Naresh Kumar Nayak

entry.title: How to Create an Analytical List Page in SAP Fiori Elements – Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
Introduction : The Analytical List Page (ALP) in SAP Fiori Elements is a powerful floorplan designed for data analysis and monitoring business performance. It combines visual charts, visual filters, and a data table within a single page, allowing users to analyze large amounts of data efficiently. The Analytical List Page enables users to quickly identify trends, filter information, and drill down into detailed data for better business insights.

Analytical List Page (ALP) in SAP Fiori Elements:

The Analytical List Page (ALP) is a SAP Fiori Elements floorplan designed for data analysis and reporting. It combines charts, filters, and tables in a single page so users can analyze business data interactively.

ALP allows users to visualize, filter, and drill down into data without creating custom UI code.

Key Features of Analytical List Page:

  1.  Visual Filter--->The filter bar allows users to filter data using graphical representations.
  2. Analytical Charts--->Charts provide a visual representation of aggregated business data.
  3. Data Table--->The table shows detailed records corresponding to the chart data.
  4. Interactive Filtering--->When a user clicks a chart segment, the table automatically filters the related records.

Flow of  Analytical List Page:

  1. CDS View
  2. Annotation
  3. Odata service
  4. SAP Fiori Elements ( Analytical List Page) Template
  5. SAP Fiori Application UI

Step 1: Create CDS View and Metadata Extension

First, create the Core Data Services (CDS) view that exposes the required business data.
After creating the CDS view, create a Metadata Extension and add the necessary UI annotations to define how the data should appear on the SAP Fiori Elements Analytical List Page.

The metadata extension is used to separate the UI annotations from the main CDS view, which helps maintain a cleaner and more structured development approach.
